Use.title: Shipping software is mostly paperwork.
Use.lede: Modern teams burn 40% of an engineering week on the choreography between commit and production. The cost is invisible to leadership but corrosive to teams.

Use.title: Twelve tools, one outage.
Use.body: A typical deploy touches CI, CD, IAM, observability, a feature-flag service, and a status page. Any one outage stops releases for everyone.

Use.title: Engineers do release engineering.
Use.body: The most expensive headcount on the team writes YAML to wire other YAML. Senior IC time is the most overpriced commodity in the stack.

Use.title: Failure mode: rollback hesitation.
Use.body: When the blast radius is unknown, teams freeze. Mean-time-to-restore stretches into hours; user-visible incidents accumulate.

VStack.Text: of dev hours sunk into pipeline maintenance & cross-tool glue.
The same survey puts the median pipeline at 11 manual touch-points between commit and prod. Every one of them is a place a senior engineer is the gatekeeper — and every one of them is automatable.
